exploration_log.aha_moment: HeaderUtilities.cs already uses `HttpRuleParser.GetTokenLength(value, 0) != value.Length` as the idiomatic token check — I just needed to replicate it.
exploration_log.hindsight: Could have jumped straight to HeaderUtilities.cs first instead of searching broadly — it's the canonical home of header parsing utilities.

alternatives: Write a custom character classification loop
Use a regex pattern for RFC 7230 tchar
key_decisions.decision: Use HttpRuleParser.GetTokenLength rather than a custom char-by-char loop
key_decisions.reasoning: HttpRuleParser is already in the project and used consistently throughout the Headers library for exactly this purpose

queries.query_text: Fix HeaderDictionary to validate header names as RFC 7230 tokens, throw ArgumentException for invalid names like spaces or delimiters, using HttpRuleParser.GetTokenLength in src/Http/Http/src/HeaderDictionary.cs

solve_notes: Added a `ThrowIfInvalidHeaderName` static method that uses the existing `HttpRuleParser.GetTokenLength` to validate that the header name is a complete RFC 7230 token. Called it from the public indexer setter right after the null and read-only checks. The `HttpRuleParser` is already linked into the project via the shared source root.
task_text: `HeaderDictionary` in `src/Http/Http/src/HeaderDictionary.cs` accepts arbitrary string keys without validating that they conform to RFC 7230 token rules (no spaces, control characters, or delimiters). Setting `headers["Invalid Header!"] = "value"` silently succeeds. Add validation in the indexer setter that rejects non-token characters with an `ArgumentException`.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: CheckValidToken
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: shows the idiomatic pattern for token validation in this codebase using HttpRuleParser.GetTokenLength

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set is sufficient for a developer who knows to look for HttpRuleParser. The thrash-preventing set adds HeaderUtilities.CheckValidToken which shows exactly how existing code validates tokens — without seeing this, an AI agent might implement a custom character loop or miss that HttpRuleParser is the right tool.
